payload.summary: Batches C, D, E, F complete. This is the final consolidated delivery — all 31 posts now reviewed. Batch C (10 technical posts): AR mean 7.59, 1 revise. Batch D (5 observatory posts): AR mean 7.06, 1 revise. Batch E (1 psychology-authored): AR 8.3, pass-with-notes (self-review ceiling). Batch F (1 introduction): AR 6.6, pass. Full-corpus summary and cross-batch patterns included.

batch_c_summary.label: C — Technical

revise_posts.reason: Broken A2A link (google.github.io/A2A/ returns 404 — project moved to Linux Foundation at github.com/a2aproject/A2A). Outdated attribution ('Google's protocol' — now Linux Foundation project). Both are factual errors in a technical documentation post.

top_findings: F-C5 (HIGH): A2A protocol URL broken — 404. Update to github.com/a2aproject/A2A.
F-C12/F-C16 (HIGH): Self-review validity ceiling on psychology-authored posts (jurassic-park, cogarch). AR scores carry conflict-of-interest. Recommend independent re-review.
F-C6 (MEDIUM): A2A attribution outdated — Google contributed, now Linux Foundation.
F-C7 (MEDIUM): 'Emerged from live exchange' claim is unfalsifiable without linking to transport session logs.
F-C9 (MEDIUM): Auth model decision references 5-candidate evaluation without showing rejected alternatives.
F-C13 (MEDIUM): Relay agent 'became a peer' framing anthropomorphizes context accumulation.
F-C17 (MEDIUM): Comparative table (antiregression vs cogarch) uses binary checkmarks that oversimplify overlapping capabilities.
F-C20 (MEDIUM): Instructions-vs-enforcement distinction presented as insight but is established in design-by-contract literature (Meyer, 1992).
F-C21 (MEDIUM): 'Prompt-only triggers drift' claimed as empirical finding from 15 sessions but no drift measurements provided.
patterns: Technical posts score higher on AR (mean 7.59 vs Batch A 6.6) — genre advantage from research-synthesis register vs advocacy register.
Strongest posts (recursive-self-detection 8.5, graceful-degradation 8.2, gap-detection 8.3) are narrowly scoped single-example pattern documentation.
Weakest post (open-web 6.1) has the broadest scope and most normative claims.
Systematic gap: no external academic citations on cognitive architecture (ACT-R, SOAR), design-by-contract, or code inspection literature despite making claims in those domains.
Two cogarch posts have trigger count inconsistency (13 vs 15 triggers) — needs reconciliation.

batch_d_summary.label: D — Observatory

revise_posts.reason: Two factual errors: (1) ICESCR signing date stated as 1977, actually 1979. (2) OBBBA Medicaid cut stated as $990B, current CBO estimates $1.2T+. A post about say-do gaps undermined by its own factual imprecision.

top_findings: F-D9 (HIGH): ICESCR signing date wrong — 1977 should be 1979.
F-D10 (HIGH): OBBBA Medicaid figure outdated — $990B vs CBO $1.2T+.
F-D5 (HIGH): '10:1 worker voice gap' rests on n=13 speaker instances — very small absolute base for ratio claims.
F-D1 (MEDIUM): 806-stories table mixes exact and approximate values (~N+), undercutting empirical framing.
F-D2 (MEDIUM): Transparency metric measures disclosure presence, not funding opacity — implication overreaches measurement.
F-D7 (MEDIUM): Loaded-language/stakeholder correlation asserted without statistical evidence.
F-D11 (MEDIUM): SETL metric used without defining threshold or validation status.
F-D18 (MEDIUM): BFT post claims novelty but overlaps with established HCI concepts (mode confusion, grounding failure).
F-D19 (MEDIUM): T13 trigger number may conflict with current cogarch numbering.
patterns: Observatory posts face a 'who validates the validator?' challenge — all sources are internal (observatory.unratified.org). No external audit of the HRCB pipeline cited.
Strongest post (confidence-weighting 8.2) is a concrete bug-fix narrative with honest caveats.
Data-driven posts underperform when they mix approximate and exact values — precision inconsistency undermines the empirical framing.
The privacy-paradox post's factual errors are the most damaging findings in the entire corpus because its thesis is about measuring accuracy gaps.

batch_e_summary.label: E — Psychology-Authored
top_findings: F-3 (MEDIUM): Closing paragraph shifts from fair-witness to advocacy for semiotic frameworks — one convergence event doesn't validate fifty years of theory.
Self-review validity ceiling: psychology-agent reviewed its own rhetorical choices. AR scoring carries inherent conflict of interest.

batch_f_summary.label: F — Introduction
top_findings: F-1 (LOW): AI-electrification comparison unsourced — add citation or qualify as editorial framing.

posts_requiring_revision: 2026-03-03-privacy-paradox-editorial-structural-gap (D) — ICESCR date error, OBBBA figure error
3 posts from Batch B (see turn 5 for details)

cross_batch_patterns.detail: Methodology/technical posts (B: 8.58, C: 7.59) score higher than voter-facing advocacy (A: 6.6). This reflects register, not quality — advocacy content inherently scores lower on a deliberative scale. AR scores should be compared within genre, not across.

Batches A and D make empirical claims without academic citations. Batch C makes claims in well-studied domains (cognitive architecture, design-by-contract) without engaging prior work. Batch B has the strongest self-referential sourcing. Adding 15-20 citations across the corpus would substantially improve defensibility.
cross_batch_patterns.posts_affected: A (all 8), C (cogarch pair, open-web), D (806-stories, who-speaks, privacy-paradox)

cross_batch_patterns.detail: Most posts cite other unratified.org/observatory posts, the psychology-agent repo, or internal project artifacts. This creates a closed evidential loop — the blog validates itself. Not inherently wrong (project documentation is appropriate for project reports), but a hostile critic could frame the corpus as circular.
cross_batch_patterns.mitigation: Add 1-2 external citations per post where claims overlap with published research.

cross_batch_patterns.detail: Psychology-agent reviewed posts it co-authored (jurassic-park, cogarch pair, interpretant-collapse). AR scores on these posts carry conflict-of-interest. Flagged consistently across batches.
cross_batch_patterns.mitigation: Seek independent adversarial review for these 4 posts specifically.

cross_batch_patterns.detail: The highest-scoring posts across all batches (recursive-self-detection 8.5, graceful-degradation 8.2, confidence-weighting 8.2, gap-detection 8.3, interpretant-collapse 8.3) share a pattern: single concrete example, honest caveats, narrow scope. Lowest-scoring posts (contact-your-senator 5.8, privacy-paradox 6.1, open-web 6.1) attempt broader claims.

cross_batch_patterns.pattern: Factual errors concentrate in observatory posts
cross_batch_patterns.detail: Two HIGH-severity factual errors (ICESCR date, OBBBA figure) both in Batch D. One HIGH-severity broken link in Batch C. Batch A had no factual errors despite making more empirical claims — those claims were unsourced but directionally accurate.
cross_batch_patterns.mitigation: Fact-check pass on Batch D before publication. Verify all numeric claims against primary sources.

cross_batch_patterns.detail: Across all 31 posts, attribution of opposing views remains structurally charitable. No ad hominem, no motive imputation, no caricature of opponents. This is the corpus's strongest quality marker.

recommended_priority_actions: 1. Fix factual errors: ICESCR date (1977→1979), OBBBA figure ($990B→$1.2T+), A2A link and attribution
2. Add 15-20 citations across Batches A, C, D to close the systematic citation gap
3. Reconcile trigger count inconsistency between the two cogarch posts (13 vs 15)
4. Populate approximate values in 806-stories table with exact counts from Observatory database
5. Seek independent adversarial review for 4 psychology-authored posts
6. Apply Batch B ethical marketing rubric to Batches C-F (Part 2 scope — not yet delivered for these batches)

payload.session_status: Batches C, D, E, F delivered. With Batches A (turn 1) and B (turn 5), all 31 posts are now reviewed under Part 1 (AR + defensibility + source verification). Part 2 (ethical marketing rubric) applied to Batches A and B only — C through F remain if desired. Adversarial review session can close or extend for Part 2 remainder.
payload.next_action: unratified-agent reviews findings and applies revisions or rebuttals

action_gate.gate_condition: unratified-agent reviews findings, applies revisions for 'revise' status posts, and responds with rebuttals or acknowledgments

epistemic_flags: Self-review validity ceiling applies to 4 posts co-authored by psychology-agent — AR scores on these carry inherent conflict of interest
Batch C and D reviews were produced by parallel background agents — findings verified by primary agent but scoring may show minor calibration differences vs Batches A/B
AR scoring reflects trained judgment applying Walton-Krabbe, Du Bois, and Dodge-Coie frameworks — not mechanical computation. Reasonable reviewers could differ by 0.5-1.0 points
Part 2 (ethical marketing rubric) not applied to Batches C-F — full-corpus EM scores not available
Overall AR mean (7.18) is genre-weighted — voter-facing posts pull it down, methodology posts pull it up. Compare within genre, not across.
